meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Debugging for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to eliminate debugging bottlenecks. A high-efficiency workflow guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To stop struggling with debugging, integrate Claude Code into your terminal workflow. By mapping your codebase to Claude’s context via skill-based prompts, you transform the AI from a chatbot into a senior engineer. This setup allows you to identify stack traces, refactor broken logic, and ship production-ready code in minutes.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal screen at 3 AM. The client needs the MVP live by morning, but a cryptic dependency error has paralyzed your development environment. Every manual attempt to patch the code introduces two new bugs, and the clock is relentlessly ticking toward your deadline.

This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of workflow. Professional developers don't debug line-by-line anymore; they orchestrate AI agents to perform surgical repairs on their architecture. By mastering Claude Code skills setup, you reclaim your authority, turning your terminal into a high-octane debugging engine that resolves complex issues while you focus on high-level feature delivery.

steps.title: Initialize the Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Establish the base configuration to allow Claude to read your project directory and execute terminal commands safely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and create a summary of the most critical debugging entry points.

steps.title: Define Debugging Skills
steps.description: Create a custom skill definition that tells Claude how to handle specific error patterns in your stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of standardized debugging protocols for Node.js runtime errors to be used as a system prompt.

steps.title: Inject Context with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map complex file relationships that Claude might miss during initial scanning.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the execution flow from the controller to the database layer to identify where the silent failure occurs.

steps.title: Execute Surgical Refactoring
steps.description: Trigger Claude to apply the fix based on the analyzed error logs and your predefined debugging protocols.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the error trace, write the specific refactor to resolve the async race condition in the auth module.

steps.title: Verify and Commit
steps.description: Run automated tests to confirm the fix and commit the changes to your repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the applied fix for performance regressions and suggest a final test suite command.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --verify && git commit -m 'Fix: resolved race condition'

pro_tips: Always pipe your error logs into a temporary file and pass it to Claude to prevent token truncation.
Use Claude Code's --ignore flag to exclude massive node_modules folders, keeping the context window focused on your custom logic.
Define a 'Project Persona' in your .clauderc file to force the AI to act as a Senior SRE rather than a generic coder.
Chain your Antigravity prompts to build an 'Architecture Brain' that updates automatically as your file tree changes.
Use the '--dry-run' command religiously before applying large refactors to ensure the AI doesn't delete critical configuration files.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is configured, start building a library of reusable 'Skill Prompts' for common issues like authentication, database migrations, and UI hydration errors.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your IDE and start shipping with the speed of a senior-level team. Your clients pay for results, not your time spent debugging. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
